creator: lukasz@wasikowski.net
text:
Unsetting USE_PERL in lang/perl5.14 prevents mail/dbmail from building. Seems that some ports assume that those perl links in /usr/bin are always present.

How-To-Repeat: Install lang/perl5.14 with OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=USE_PERL
Try to install mail/dbmail, it ends with:

# tail -n 20 /poudriere/poudriere_data/logs/bulk/usunto/default/dbmail-3.0.2_5.log
gmake[2]: Entering directory `/wrkdirs/usr/ports/mail/dbmail/work/dbmail-3.0.2/test'
gmake[2]: Nothing to be done for `all'.
gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/wrkdirs/usr/ports/mail/dbmail/work/dbmail-3.0.2/test'
Making all in man
gmake[2]: Entering directory `/wrkdirs/usr/ports/mail/dbmail/work/dbmail-3.0.2/man'
asciidoc --unsafe -b docbook -d manpage dbmail.txt
xmlto man dbmail.xml 2>/dev/null
./fixsp.pl dbmail.1
gmake[2]: ./fixsp.pl: Command not found
gmake[2]: *** [dbmail.1] Error 127
rm dbmail.xml
gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/wrkdirs/usr/ports/mail/dbmail/work/dbmail-3.0.2/man'
gmake[1]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1
gmake[1]: Leaving directory `/wrkdirs/usr/ports/mail/dbmail/work/dbmail-3.0.2'
gmake: *** [all] Error 2
*** [do-build] Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/mail/dbmail.
===>  Cleaning for dbmail-3.0.2_5
build of /usr/ports/mail/dbmail ended at Fri Mar 29 11:17:25 CET 2013
